content: Just as clinical memory and reflection modules help humans diagnose and fix specific memory breakdowns by formalizing the three-stage encoding-storing-retrieval process, autonomous goal-seeking agents can be redesigned to embed a dedicated self-reflection memory module that actively identifies retrieval failures instead of passively storing unstructured data. This design would let agents iteratively repair broken memory pathways the same way humans work through cognitive memory impairments to improve long-term task performance.
